methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $19M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $29.0B.

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Aeon Industrial is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Naweed Tahmas that has raised $18.6M to date. The valuation analysis references a peer universe of 10 comparables—2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, and Flock Safety—spanning Seed and Series A stages within the same sector.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis uses the $18.6M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $46M. Peer-Median analysis examines five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $60M (Tenna Systems, Seed) to $29.0B (Scale AI, Series A), with a median of $300M. This dispersion reflects material heterogeneity within the peer set, though the median is driven by later-stage companies.

The two methods produce a triangulated range of $40M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $173M. The $46M stage-progression result anchors the lower bound, while the peer median at $300M anchors the upper range. Key uncertainties include execution risk relative to more mature peers, the applicability of later-stage (Series A) comparables to an early-stage company, and the extreme valuation spread within the peer universe, which limits precision around any single point estimate.
